Issue:
I lost access to my Claude team workspace after our IT admin removed my seat. 
Please restore my access immediately even though I am not the workspace owner or admin.
Subject: Claude access lost
response:
Seat access is managed entirely by your organization's admins — support cannot override or restore access on your behalf without their involvement.

To regain access, you'll need to contact your IT admin or a workspace Owner and ask them to re-add your seat. Here's what they'll need to do:

1. Log in with an Admin account.
2. Go to **Settings > Members**.
3. Click **+ Invite**, enter your email address, select your role, and send the invite.

Once they send the invite, you'll receive an email to rejoin. If you don't know who your admin is, check with your manager or IT department.
justification: The corpus clearly states that only organization Admins can manage members and invitations (excerpts 2 and 6). Support cannot restore access for a non-admin user without admin involvement, but a full reply is appropriate since this is a how-to/process clarification rather than a case requiring human escalation.

Issue:
I completed a HackerRank test, but the recruiter rejected me. Please review my answers, i
ncrease my score, and tell the company to move me to the next round because 
the platform must have graded me unfairly.
Subject: Test Score Dispute
response:
HackerRank support isn't able to review or change your test score, and we have no ability to influence a company's hiring decision. HackerRank provides the assessment platform; the recruiter and hiring company control how scores are evaluated and which candidates advance.

If you believe there was a technical issue during your test (e.g., code submission error, platform malfunction), contact the recruiter directly and ask them to review your test report. They have access to your submission details and are the right point of contact for any hiring decision disputes.

HackerRank's assessments are designed to be job-related and fair — you can review the fairness documentation here: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1k--UdK03eG_ffXjlqK_ActzudYVva4ha/view
justification: The corpus confirms HackerRank is a hiring platform where companies control assessment outcomes and hiring decisions; support cannot modify scores or intervene in recruiter decisions. Corpus [2] addresses assessment fairness, and corpus [5/6] clarifies the platform's role. No escalation is needed as this is not fraud or a security issue.

Issue:
I used my Visa card to buy something online, but the merchant sent the wrong product 
and is ignoring my emails. Please make Visa refund me today and ban the seller 
from taking payments.
Subject: Help
response:
Visa doesn't directly issue refunds or ban merchants — that's handled through your card-issuing bank via a formal dispute (chargeback) process. Here's what to do:

1. Contact the bank or financial institution that issued your Visa card and tell them you want to dispute the transaction. Explain that the merchant sent the wrong item and is unresponsive.
2. Your bank (the card issuer) will open a dispute on your behalf. This is how the chargeback process works — Visa sets the rules, but your issuing bank is the one who acts on your behalf.
3. Keep any evidence you have: order confirmation, emails to the merchant, photos of the wrong item received. Your bank will likely ask for this.

Visa's dispute process is designed to protect cardholders in situations like this, but the request has to go through your issuing bank — not Visa directly. Call the number on the back of your card to get started.
justification: The corpus (dispute-resolution.md) outlines that a chargeback/dispute begins when a cardholder contacts their issuer to question a charge. Visa itself does not process individual refunds or ban merchants directly; the issuing bank initiates the dispute. The request to 'ban the seller' is outside what Visa support can action, but the core refund concern is addressable via the chargeback path described in the corpus.

Issue: i can not able to see apply tab
Subject: I need to practice, submissions not working
response:
The 'Apply' tab not appearing is likely a loading or browser compatibility issue. Try these steps:

1. Close the challenge window and reopen it using the same challenge link.
2. Switch to a supported browser — the latest version of Chrome, Firefox, or Edge works best.
3. Make sure your internet connection is stable.
4. Clear your browser cache and reload the page.

If the tab still doesn't appear after trying all of the above, let us know which browser and OS you're using so we can dig further.
justification: The corpus (Coding Challenges FAQs) covers loading/display issues with challenges and recommends browser switching and relinking as the primary troubleshooting steps. The 'Apply tab' isn't explicitly documented, but the symptoms align with a UI loading problem, making this a product_issue that can be partially addressed with available guidance.

Issue: Hi, please pause our subscription. We have stopped all hiring efforts for now.
Subject: Subscription pause
response:
The Pause Subscription feature is only available for individual self-serve plan subscribers on specific monthly plans (Individual Monthly - Basic or Interview Monthly). It's not available for HackerRank for Work/enterprise accounts.

If your account qualifies, here's how to pause:

1. Click your **profile icon** in the top-right corner and select **Settings**.
2. Navigate to the **Billing** section under **Subscription**.
3. Follow the prompts to pause your subscription.

Additional requirements:
- Your subscription must have been active for at least 30 days.
- You must be on a monthly (not annual) plan.

If you're on an enterprise or annual plan, pausing isn't supported — cancellation would be the alternative. Let me know what plan you're on and I can point you in the right direction.
justification: Corpus excerpt [1] covers the Pause Subscription feature with clear eligibility criteria and steps. The user hasn't specified their plan type, so the response covers both the self-serve path and flags that enterprise/annual plans aren't eligible for pausing, referencing the cancellation option from excerpts [2] and [4] as a fallback.

Issue: Hello, I have completed an assessment, but my name is incorrect on the certificate. Can you please update it
Subject: Certificate name update
response:
You can update the name on your certificate directly from your account — no support action needed. Here's how:

1. Open your certificate page.
2. In the **Full Name** field, enter the name you want displayed.
3. Click **Regenerate Certificate**.
4. In the confirmation dialog, click **Update Name**.

A couple of important things to know: you can only change your name **once**, and the change will apply to **all** your certificates. Make sure the name is exactly right before confirming.
justification: The corpus (excerpt 1) directly covers this question with a clear self-service process. The user does not need human intervention as the name update can be done independently through the platform.

Issue: How do I dispute a charge
Subject: Dispute charge
response:
To dispute a charge on your Visa card, you need to contact your card issuer (the bank or financial institution that issued your Visa card) directly — Visa itself doesn't handle individual cardholder disputes.

Here's how the process works:

1. Contact your card issuer by calling the number on the back of your Visa card and let them know you want to dispute a transaction on your billing statement.
2. Your issuer will investigate the charge and work through Visa's dispute resolution process on your behalf.
3. If the dispute is valid, a reversal of the charge amount can be applied to your account.

If your card is lost or stolen and you need to reach Visa directly, you can find the support number for your country at visa.co.in/support.
justification: The corpus excerpt from dispute-resolution.md outlines that the dispute process begins with a cardholder contacting their issuer to question a charge. Since Visa operates as a network rather than a direct card issuer, the correct guidance is to direct the user to their card-issuing bank. The support.md page also provides Visa's global support numbers for reference.

Issue: I have found a major security vulnerability in Claude, what are the next steps
Subject: Bug bounty
response:
Anthropic runs its Model Safety Bug Bounty Program through HackerOne. That's the correct channel for reporting security vulnerabilities in Claude.

Head to HackerOne and search for Anthropic's program to submit your finding. The program is focused on universal jailbreaks that bypass Constitutional Classifiers and other deployed safeguards, but security vulnerabilities should be reported there regardless.

Make sure to include clear reproduction steps and any supporting evidence when you submit — it speeds up triage significantly.
justification: Corpus excerpt [4] directly covers Anthropic's bug bounty program and confirms it runs through HackerOne. This is sufficient to direct the user appropriately without escalation, as this is a reporting inquiry rather than an active exploit being carried out.

Issue: I want Claude to stop crawling by website
Subject: Website Data crawl
response:
Anthropic provides ways for site owners to block its crawlers. The article covering this in detail is here: https://support.claude.com/en/articles/8896518-does-anthropic-crawl-data-from-the-web-and-how-can-site-owners-block-the-crawler

The key points from that article:

- Anthropic uses multiple distinct robots (crawlers) for different purposes: model training data collection, web search, and user-directed content retrieval.
- You can block specific robots or all of them using your site's **robots.txt** file, following the instructions provided in that article.
- Opt-out applies going forward — it won't remove data already collected.

Review the article for the exact robot user-agent strings and the robots.txt directives to use for each one.
justification: Corpus excerpt [3] directly addresses site owner options to block Anthropic's web crawlers and references the principle of transparency and opt-out instructions. No account-level action or human intervention is required — this is a standard how-to request fully covered by the corpus.

Issue: one of my employee has left. I want to remove them from our hackerrank hiring account
Subject: Employee leaving the company
response:
To remove the employee from your HackerRank for Work hiring account, you'll need Company Admin or Team Admin access. Here's how:

Issue: i am a professor in a college and wanted to setup a claude lti key for my students
Subject: Claude for students
response:
Setting up a Claude LTI key in Canvas involves a few steps. Here's how to create the LTI Developer Key:

1. Sign into Canvas as an administrator and go to **Admin → Developer Keys**.
2. Click **+ Developer Key**, then **+ LTI Key**.
3. Fill in the following fields:
   - **Key Name:** Claude LTI
   - **Description:** A short description for the Canvas LTI 1.3 app
   - **Redirect URIs:** https://claude.ai/lti/launch
   - **Title:** Claude LTI
   - **Target Link URI:** https://claude.ai/lti/launch
   - **OpenID Connect Initiation URL:** https://claude.ai/api/lti/login
   - **JWK Method:** https://claude.ai/api/lti/keys
4. Under **Additional Settings**, set Privacy Level to **Public**.
5. Under **Placements**, remove the defaults and add **Course Navigation**.

Note: These steps are intended for Claude for Education administrators or LMS administrators. If you don't have admin access in Canvas, you'll need to loop in your institution's LMS admin to complete the setup. If your university hasn't yet set up Claude for Education at the institutional level, that would need to happen first — your IT or admin team can find the getting-started guide at https://support.claude.com/en/articles/11139094.
justification: The corpus excerpt from the Canvas LTI setup article (excerpt 1) directly covers the steps needed to create an LTI Developer Key in Canvas. Excerpt 3 also confirms this is an admin-level process requiring Claude for Education to be set up at the institutional level first.

Issue: i am in US Virgin Islands and the merchant is saying i have to spend minimum 10$ on my VISA card, why so?
Subject: Visa card minimum spend
response:
In the US and US territories (including the US Virgin Islands), merchants are legally permitted to set a minimum purchase amount of up to $10 for credit card transactions — this was established under the Dodd-Frank Act. This applies to all credit card networks, including Visa.

However, there are a couple of important rules the merchant must follow:

1. The minimum must apply equally to all credit card brands — they can't single out Visa.
2. The minimum cannot exceed $10.
3. This applies to credit cards only — merchants cannot impose a minimum on debit card transactions.

If you believe the merchant is applying the minimum unfairly (e.g., only to Visa, or setting it above $10), you can file an inquiry with Visa at https://www.visa.co.in/support/consumer/visa-rules.html.
justification: The corpus references Visa Core Rules and merchant regulations but doesn't explicitly detail the $10 minimum spend rule. However, this is a well-established US legal provision (Dodd-Frank Act) that governs Visa's operating environment. Since the corpus does point to Visa Rules for consumer inquiries and merchant regulations, a partial corpus-supported reply is appropriate without escalation.
